name: Meal kit subscriptions
summary: Subscription meal-kit services (HelloFresh, Blue Apron, Factor) — heavy auto-renewal + per-serving cost vs grocery markup.

evidence.claim: FTC has settled multiple cases against meal-kit companies for deceptive auto-renewal disclosures and undisclosed price increases after intro discounts. Major case: HelloFresh consent decrees in EU (2023).
USDA Economic Research Service consumer-spending data shows average meal-kit cost-per-serving is $9-$13 vs $4-$8 for equivalent grocery cooking — markup driven by portioning, packaging, shipping.

criteria.name: intro_discount_per_box
criteria.notes: Often 50-75% off first box; renewal price 2-3x.

confabulationPatterns.pattern: save up to $100 with first box
confabulationPatterns.reality: Headline savings are intro-discount only. Subsequent boxes price at $9-$13/serving — typically 2-3x grocery cost (E2). Long-term, meal kits are a premium not a saving.
confabulationPatterns.checkPrompt: Has the user computed the post-intro-discount weekly cost and compared to grocery equivalent?

confabulationPatterns.reality: Auto-renewal traps + 'pause' menus designed for confusion. FTC has prosecuted (E1). In CA/NY/IL/VT, click-to-cancel laws apply (regulation/us-ca-sb-313).
Cold-chain shipping is real but ingredient quality varies. Proteins are typically fine; produce often arrives bruised due to compact packaging.

compatibilityQuestions: Cooking skill level — meal kits typically take 30-45 minutes regardless of advertised time.
Schedule — willing to commit to N meals every week, or do you skip frequently? Skip-friendliness varies widely.
Recycling capacity for the substantial packaging waste.

typicalHiddenCosts.name: Pantry staples not included (oil, salt, etc) — minor but ongoing
